title: Rufacer drummondii
taxonomy: Rufacer drummondii is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is a perennial that grows to be around 6-12 inches tall, with small blue or white flowers. Rufacer drummondii is also known as Drummond's beardtongue or Drummond's penstemon. The classification of this plant is further defined by its botanical name, which is part of the larger classification system used in botany.
uses: Rufacer drummondii, also known as Drummond's rockcress, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are used to treat colds and fever by making a tea from them. Rufacer drummondii has also been reported to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. Additionally, it has been used to treat digestive issues and as a treatment for respiratory problems. More research is needed to fully confirm its medicinal uses and effectiveness.

intro: Rufacer drummondii, also known as Drummond's skullcap, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Rufacer drummondii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
